data.title: Allurion to raise $6.1 million in direct offering and private placement
data.description:
• Allurion Technologies (NYSE:ALUR) on Wednesday said it has entered into a definitive securities purchase agreement with certain institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 900,000 shares of its common stock at $5.23 per share in a registered direct offering.
  • In addition, the company will issue to the investors warrants to purchase up to 1.8 million shares of common stock in a concurrent private placement. The warrants have an exercise price of $5.23 per share and will become exercisable immediately following the date of stockholder approval and expire on the fifth anniversary of receipt of such approval.
  • The company also entered into a subscription agreement with funds affiliated with Leavitt Equity Partners for the purchase and sale of 267,686 shares of common stock and warrants to purchase up to 535,372 shares of common stock in a concurrent private placement at a purchase price of $5.23 per share and accompanying warrant.
  • The aggregate gross proceeds to the company from the offering and concurrent private placement are expected to be approximately $6.1 million.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering and concurrent private placement to fund its clinical pipeline testing the effects of the combination of the Allurion Balloon and GLP-1 therapy on muscle mass and long-term GLP-1 adherence, for working capital, and for general corporate purposes.
  • The closing of the offering and concurrent private placement is expected to occur on or about February 20, 2025.
  • ALUR shares fall over 6% premarket to $5.23.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Wednesday: TPR, ADI, MANU and CVNA
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were muted on Wednesday, as the holiday-shortened week came closer to winding down. Here are some stocks to watch on Wednesday:

  • Shares of Tapestry (NYSE:TPR) were in focus, flat in pre-market trading, after the company said it would be selling its iconic Stuart Weitzman brand to footwear firm Caleres (CAL) for $105M in cash. The move comes after Tapestry (NYSE:TPR) last year was forced to terminate its proposed $8.5B takeover of Capri (CPRI) due to regulatory pushbacks, in a deal that would have seen household fashion brands such as Coach, Versace, Jimmy Choo and Michael Kors under a combined company. "We are pleased that we found Stuart Weitzman a home in Caleres – an ideal owner to guide its next chapter of growth,” TPR top boss Joanne Crevoiserat said.
  • Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) stock added more than 5% ahead of the opening bell, after the Wilmington, Mass.-based company delivered a FQ1 2025 top- and bottom-line beat and provided current quarter guidance that also surpassed expectations. Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) makes chips that power everything from amplifiers to audio and video products, and also provides other products such as converters, Ethernet solutions and sensors. "Our recovery is being propelled by improving cyclical dynamics and numerous new wins across our franchise converting to revenue," CEO Vincent Roche said.
  • Class A shares of Manchester United (NYSE:MANU) were slightly higher ahead of market open, even as the famed soccer club swung to a quarterly loss of £27.7M ($34.9M). The bottom-line was hurt by a 42.1% decline in broadcasting revenue, reflecting the club's participation in the less lucrative UEFA Europa League rather than the Champions League. “We recognize the challenges in improving our men’s team’s league position and we are all working hard, collectively, to achieve that," chief executive Omar Berrada said. For fiscal 2025, MANU reiterated its previous revenue guidance of £650M to £670M.
  • Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) stock will garner attention, ahead of the used car retailer's Q4 report after hours. Wall Street expects the CVNA to earn 32 cents per share on revenue of $3.34B. Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) runs an e-commerce platform for buying and selling used cars, and is best known for its car vending machines. The heavily shorted stock is having a strong year so far, up about 40% YTD. CVNA top boss Ernie Garcia had heavily touted the company's Q3 performance, noting "record performance in virtually every key financial measure." He called CVNA the "fastest growing and most profitable automotive retailer."

data.title: AXIL Brands signs licensing agreement with Feld Entertainment for branded hearing protection products
data.description:
• AXIL Brands (OTCQB:AXIL) said on Wednesday that it has entered into a multi-year licensing agreement with Feld Entertainment and Monster Jam, for a Monster Jam-branded hearing protection product portfolio.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, AXIL will produce and sell new adult and youth lines of Monster Jam-branded earbuds, earmuffs, hearing devices, and ear plugs in distribution channels in the United States.
  • The new product lines will feature the signature AXIL sound quality, hearing protection, and enhancement functionality designed for fans of all ages. They are anticipated to be available for purchase in the spring of 2025 at various shops, retail outlets, and online platforms.
  • AXIL Brands (OTCQB:AXIL) shares were up more than 8% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Prologis announces CEO retirement, appointment of a successor
data.description:
• Industrial REIT Prologis (NYSE:PLD) announced on Wednesday the retirement of CEO Hamid Moghadam, and the appointment of President Dan Letter as a successor.
  • PLD shares were trading -2.68% pre-market at $118.06.
  • Co-founder Moghadam is set to retire from the CEO role, effective January 1, 2026. Moghadam will continue as executive chairman.
  • Letter, who has been with Prologis since 2004, will join the company's board immediately.

data.title: Bristol Opdivo + chemo combo shows overall survival benefit in NSCLC
data.description:
• A combination of Bristol Myers Squibb's Opdivo (nivolumab) and platinum-doublet chemotherapy led to a clinically meaningful improvement in overall survival compared to chemo alone in patients with non-small cell lung cancer, according to a new analysis.
  • The final analysis of overall survival in the phase 3 CheckMate -816 study was in patients with resectable NSCLC.
  • Overall survival was a secondary endpoint. The pharma previously reported that primary endpoints, event-free survival and pathological complete response, were also met.

data.title: APi raises FY24 revenue guidance to $7B
data.description:
• APi Group (NYSE:APG) said on Wednesday that the company's net revenues for 2024 will be above its October 31, 2024 guide of about $7B.
  • The company also expects 2024 adjusted EBITDA to be in-line with the midpoint of its guided range of $890 to $900 million. Additionally, the company expects to end the year with a net leverage ratio below our target of 2.5x.
  • Consensus for FY24 revenue is 6.99B.
  • Net revenues for 2025 is expected to range between $7.3B to $7.5B. Meanwhile, adjusted EBITDA is expected to range between $970M to $1B.
  • APi also announced the repricing of its term loan due 2029. The repricing reduces the applicable margin on all outstanding amounts by 25 basis points and represents cash savings of approximately $5 million annually.
  • Additionally, APi said that it repaid $100 million of its term loan due 2029 on December 31, 2024, leaving $2.1B outstanding. APi’s aggregate $1.8B of interest rate swaps related to its term loan remain in effect following the repricing.
  • APG -6.56% premarket to $35.0.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Predictive Oncology announces registered direct offering; shares fall
data.description:
• Predictive Oncology (NASDAQ:POAI) has entered into definitive agreements for the purchase and sale of 363,336 shares of common stock at $1.50/share in a registered direct offering.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about February 19, 2025.
  • The gross proceeds to the company from the offering are expected to be $545,004.
  • POAI intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • POAI shares slump near 16% premarket on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release
